what_input_feature: The early option-selector bytes (the `option` value switched on at setopt.c:197) must decode to a CURLOPT value that matches NO enumerated case, so control falls through every `case` to the `default:` arm at line 3110.
why_winner_satisfies: naive (winner) seeds carry varied selector bytes at offset 0x0001 (2f, 01, 06, 05) and hit the default arm 6 times with the W set reaching `default:` exclusively (line 3110 marked [W]); plain havoc byte mutation lands on arbitrary, non-enumerated option values with no force pulling them onto a valid case.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser) seeds are pinned to selector byte 0x01 at offset 0x0001 (all 19 instances), keeping `option` on an enumerated case so they never reach default; I2SRandReplace substitutes the very CURLOPT case-constants the switch compares against back into the input, steering the option toward a matching case and away from the unknown-option default.
mechanism_attribution: The decisive I2S technique HANDICAPS the loser: cmplog's CMP-buffer-driven input-to-state substitution keeps copying enumerated case-constants into the selector, so the option keeps matching a known case. The winner naive has no comparison logging and wins by plain havoc byte mutation that drifts the selector onto an arbitrary, non-enumerated value, taking default. The winner-enabling mechanism is havoc-only.
